question: Why is my EKS payment service timing out?
ground_truth: Pod evictions caused by memory pressure from the daily-report-generator CronJob led to orphaned RDS connections and connection pool exhaustion, causing timeouts.

question: How do I restart a crashed pod in Kubernetes?
ground_truth: Delete the pod with kubectl delete pod <name> -n <namespace>. The ReplicaSet will automatically create a replacement pod.

question: What causes CrashLoopBackOff in Kubernetes?
ground_truth: CrashLoopBackOff occurs when a container repeatedly fails to start. Common causes: application error, missing config/secret, OOMKill, or incorrect command in the container spec.

question: How do I scale a Kubernetes deployment?
ground_truth: Use kubectl scale deployment <name> --replicas=<n> or update the replicas field in the Deployment manifest and apply with kubectl apply -f.

question: What is a PodDisruptionBudget and when should I use it?
ground_truth: A PodDisruptionBudget limits voluntary disruptions to pods. Use it to ensure minimum availability during node drains or rolling updates by specifying minAvailable or maxUnavailable.

question: How do I check RDS connection pool usage?
ground_truth: Query CloudWatch metric DatabaseConnections for your RDS instance. Alert threshold is 80% of max_connections parameter. Connection pool exhaustion causes application timeouts.

question: What is the EKS node instance type for worker nodes?
ground_truth: Worker nodes run on c5.4xlarge instances (16 vCPU, 32GB RAM). The H100 GPU instance (p4d family) is dedicated to vLLM inference only.

question: How does the Planner-Executor-Critic workflow work?
ground_truth: The PEC workflow: Planner creates an investigation plan, Executor runs each step calling tools (CloudWatch, Jira, kubectl), Critic validates completeness and triggers replan if gaps found. Max 2 replans.

question: What monitoring dashboards are available?
ground_truth: Three Grafana dashboards: Data Health (DVC commits, embedding drift, QA coverage), MLflow Metrics (RAGAS scores, latency trends, Katib HPO), and Platform Performance (throughput, workflow routing, tool calls).

question: How do I trigger the P09 RAGAS evaluation pipeline?
ground_truth: P09 triggers automatically post-staging deployment and weekly on Monday 03:00 UTC. Manual trigger via Kubeflow UI or run_pipelines.ps1 -Pipeline p09 -QaLimit 50.

question: What is the hallucination rate target for the LLM platform?
ground_truth: Target hallucination rate is below 2% (0.02). Measured by RAGAS as 1 - faithfulness. Hard block at 0.15 in the P09 deployment gate.

question: How is the vLLM server configured?
ground_truth: vLLM serves Llama 2 7B GPTQ on an H100 (80GB HBM) using PagedAttention and continuous batching. Target throughput is 80+ tokens/second. OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint.

question: What is the DVC remote storage location?
ground_truth: DVC remote is S3 bucket llm-platform-dvc-remote in us-east-1 with Intelligent-Tiering lifecycle policy. Configure with: dvc remote add -d s3remote s3://llm-platform-dvc-remote.

question: How do I check if a Kubernetes node is under memory pressure?
ground_truth: Run kubectl describe node <name> and look for MemoryPressure condition. Also check kubectl top nodes for memory utilization. Nodes under pressure trigger pod evictions.

question: What Qdrant collections exist in the platform?
ground_truth: Three collections: tech_docs (infrastructure runbooks, incident history), hr_policies (leave, benefits, code of conduct), org_info (team structures, contacts, processes). All use 384-dimensional cosine vectors.

question: How is data versioned in the LLM platform?
ground_truth: DVC 3.x tracks all datasets, golden QA pairs, and model checkpoints with SHA-based content addressing backed by S3. Every data change is committed to Git as a .dvc pointer file.

question: What triggers the automated retraining pipeline P11?
ground_truth: P11 is triggered by: Evidently AI drift alert (JS-divergence > 0.15 or 10% faithfulness drop), P09 RAGAS gate failure, or manual trigger. Runs Katib HPO then RAGAS evaluation before promoting to Staging.

question: What is the rate limit for the LLM platform API?
ground_truth: 50 requests per minute per client IP. Enforced by Redis sliding window rate limiter. Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429. Rate limit counters reset automatically after the 60-second window.

question: How do I check CloudWatch logs for the payment service?
ground_truth: Use CloudWatch Logs Insights on log group /aws/eks/llm-platform/application with filter: fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /payment/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 20

question: What is the MLflow experiment for RAGAS results?
ground_truth: RAGAS results are logged to MLflow experiment ragas-evaluation with runs tagged pipeline=P09-RAGAS. Metrics: faithfulness, answer_relevancy, context_precision, context_recall, answer_correctness, hallucination_rate.
